eval-discount-trap.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa absent, Myntra shows subsidized liquidation, Meesho shows price-driven volume. Demand is price-sensitive, not style-driven.

conflicts.title: Nykaa absent vs Myntra strong (discount-driven) vs Meesho strong (price-driven)
conflicts.nykaa_says: No premium demand; style not present at full price.
conflicts.myntra_says: High rank but demand is subsidized liquidation; discount is the driver.
conflicts.meesho_says: Strong volume at low price; price-sensitive demand.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is there any genuine style demand beyond deep discounts and low prices?
conflicts.proves: Demand exists only at heavy discount or low price points.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove the style has standalone appeal at moderate or full price.

convergences.title: Both Myntra and Meesho show strong presence
convergences.detail: Myntra has high rank and reviews; Meesho has high volume. Both indicate market activity.
convergences.proves: The style has broad market exposure and some sales traction.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove the traction is due to style preference rather than price/discount.

eval-discount-trap.upside_summary: The style has strong visibility on Myntra (rank 3) and high volume on Meesho (8000+ units). If the discount is temporary, there may be latent demand that could be captured at a lower price point.
eval-discount-trap.catch_summary: Nykaa's absence suggests no premium demand. Myntra's demand is subsidized liquidation (65% off, 3.2 rating). Meesho's volume is at ₹299. The style appears to be a discount trap with no genuine style signal.

myntra.reason: Strong presence but heavily distorted by subsidized liquidation; review velocity down-weighted 75%.

meesho.reason: Strong volume but low price and moderate rating; no reseller growth acceleration.

eval-discount-trap.bet_rationale: All external signals are distorted by price/discount. No clean demand signal exists. Nykaa's absence confirms no premium interest. Wait for clearer evidence of style-driven demand.
eval-discount-trap.key_uncertainty: Would the style sell at a moderate price (e.g., ₹599-799) without deep discount?

Myntra discount drops below 40% with stable rank
Internal POS test shows sell-through at moderate price
missing_evidence: Live marketplace data for price comparison
eval-discount-trap.demand_integrity: Subsidized Liquidation on Myntra; price-driven on Meesho. Overall integrity low.

eval-premium-mirage.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa shows strong premium demand but Meesho absent — style may be premium-only, not mass-market viable

conflicts.title: Nykaa strong premium presence vs Meesho completely absent
conflicts.nykaa_says: Strong demand at ₹1850 full price with high ratings
conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate demand at 15% discount, strategic pricing
conflicts.meesho_says: Zero presence — no demand in value-fashion segment

convergences.title: Clean demand signals at premium and mass retail
convergences.detail: Nykaa full-price and Myntra low-discount products both show good ratings and reviews, indicating genuine style preference rather than discount-driven demand
convergences.proves: The style has intrinsic appeal at higher price points

eval-premium-mirage.upside_summary: The style shows strong premium demand at full price on Nykaa and moderate demand on Myntra with strategic pricing. Ratings and reviews are solid, suggesting genuine customer satisfaction. If the trend broadens, it could capture a loyal premium segment.
eval-premium-mirage.catch_summary: Meesho absence is a major red flag — the style has zero traction in price-sensitive markets. This may be a premium-only trend with limited total addressable market. Internal POS has no prior data to validate, so any buy is speculative.

myntra.reason: Moderate presence with clean strategic pricing, no noise flags

convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand at premium price points or in own stores.

eval-mass-only.upside_summary: Meesho shows explosive volume (52k+ units) with accelerating reseller growth, indicating strong value demand. Myntra also shows moderate demand with decent reviews. The basic cotton kurti category has proven appeal in mass channels.
eval-mass-only.catch_summary: Nykaa's absence suggests no premium demand. Myntra's demand is discount-dependent (35% off) with mediocre rating (3.5★). The trend may be purely price-driven, not style-driven. No internal POS data to validate.

eval-golden-convergence.headline: All sources converge: Golden Kurti shows strong, clean demand across premium and value segments with no discount distortion

convergences.title: Strong demand across all channels
convergences.detail: Nykaa (premium), Myntra (mass retail), and Meesho (value) all show strong presence with low discount risk and positive ratings
convergences.proves: Broad-based demand for golden kurtis across price tiers
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether the same style appeals to all segments or different products

convergences.title: Low discount distortion
convergences.detail: Nykaa at 5% off, Myntra at 10% off (Strategic Value Pricing), Meesho at likely low discount given high velocity
convergences.cannot_prove: Sustainability of demand at full price in premium segment

eval-golden-convergence.upside_summary: Golden Kurti shows strong, clean demand across premium (Nykaa), mass retail (Myntra), and value (Meesho) channels with minimal discount distortion. Myntra's rank 2 and 3200 reviews indicate broad appeal, while Meesho's 28,000+ units and accelerating reseller growth suggest untapped tier-2/3/4 potential.
eval-golden-convergence.catch_summary: No internal POS data exists to validate external signals. The price gap between Nykaa (₹1450) and Meesho (₹349) raises questions about whether the same product can succeed across such different price points. Premium demand may be limited to niche buyers.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence, low discount, high rating, but limited product count (2)

myntra.reason: Strong presence, low discount, high review count and velocity, no noise flags

meesho.reason: Strong presence, high units sold, accelerating reseller growth, good rating

internal.reason: No prior data available; cannot confirm or deny

eval-golden-convergence.bet_rationale: All three external sources show strong, clean demand with low discount distortion, indicating genuine style preference. Expand to multiple stores/regions to capture broad demand across price tiers.
eval-golden-convergence.key_uncertainty: Will the same golden kurti design appeal to both premium (₹1450) and value (₹349) customers, or are these different products?

eval-silent-meesho.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa shows strong premium demand, but Meesho absent and Myntra only moderate — demand may be niche, not mass

conflicts.title: Nykaa strong premium presence vs Meesho absent price-sensitive demand
conflicts.nykaa_says: Strong demand at ₹2200 with zero discount, high ratings
conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate demand at 20% off, rank 18, good reviews
conflicts.meesho_says: No products, no resellers, no sales
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this a premium-only trend that won't scale to value-fashion, or is Meesho simply not listing it yet?
conflicts.proves: Premium demand exists; mass-market demand is unproven
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether mass-market demand will emerge or is absent permanently

convergences.title: Clean demand signals at premium and mass retail
convergences.detail: Nykaa shows zero-discount demand at ₹2200; Myntra shows strategic pricing at 20% off with good ratings. Both indicate genuine style preference, not discount-driven.
convergences.proves: The style has appeal at higher price points with minimal discount distortion
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether this appeal extends to price-sensitive segments

eval-silent-meesho.upside_summary: The niche kurti shows strong premium demand on Nykaa with zero discount and high ratings, and moderate demand on Myntra with strategic pricing. This suggests a genuine style preference among quality-conscious buyers, with potential for healthy margins if positioned correctly.
eval-silent-meesho.catch_summary: Meesho's complete absence indicates no price-sensitive demand, and Myntra's moderate presence (rank 18) is not overwhelming. The trend may be too niche for broad scaling, and internal POS has no prior data to validate. Capital commitment should be cautious.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence, zero discount, high ratings, clean signal

myntra.reason: Moderate presence, strategic pricing, clean discount context, no noise flags

eval-silent-meesho.bet_rationale: Premium demand is confirmed but mass-market demand is unproven. A small trial in premium-focused stores can validate real sell-through without overcommitting.
eval-silent-meesho.key_uncertainty: Will Meesho show demand if the product is listed there, or is the style inherently premium-only?
